zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth Baxter於從1676年到1677年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可時，她的父親George Baxter27歲，母親Mary Baxter11674歲。她於大約1699年與John Martin在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1750年在美國維吉尼亞州斯特費爾德斯特費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Baxter于从1676年到1677年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可时，她的父亲George Baxter27岁，母亲Mary Baxter11674岁。她于大约1699年与John Martin在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1750年在美国弗吉尼亚州斯特費爾德斯特費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Baxter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

variants.name: Kempster
meanings: English: occupational name for a wool or flax comber, MiddleEnglish kem(be)stere (an agent derivative of Old Englishcemban ‘to comb’). Although this was originally a feminine formof the masculine kembere, by the Middle English period thesuffix -stre had lost its feminine force, and the term was usedto refer to both sexes. Compare Baxter , Brewster , Dexter .

variants.name: Baker
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Baxter was born from 1676 to 1677, in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, George Baxter, was 27 and her mother, Mary Baxter, was 11674. She married John Martin about 1699, in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1750, in Chesterfield, Chesterfield, Virginia, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Baxter(이)가 1676년 부터 1677년 까지 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Baxter(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Baxter(은)는 11674세였다. 그녀는 대략 1699년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에서 John Martin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1750년 미국 버지니아 주 체스터필드 체스터필드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Baxter nasceu de 1676 a 1677, em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, George Baxter, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Mary Baxter, tinha 11674 anos. Ela casou-se com John Martin aproximadamente 1699, em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1750, em Chesterfield, Chesterfield, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Baxter родилась с 1676 по 1677 в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени George Baxter было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Baxter, 11674. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Martin около 1699 г. в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1750 г. в местности честирфиелд, честирфиелд, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Elizabeth Baxter nació desde 1676 hasta 1677, en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, George Baxter, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Mary Baxter, tenía 11674 años. Se casó con John Martin aproximadamente 1699, en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1750, en Chesterfield, Chesterfield, Virginia, Estados Unidos.
